stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 4 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 20 observations with mean 49.7 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.4). region B has 20 observations with mean 65.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 6.3). region C has 10 observations with mean 55.6 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.7). region D has 8 observations with mean 57.4 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 8.2). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 5.8 and 14.4 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region C: 55.6 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 55.6). Range [55.6, 55.7] as between-region SD varies from 5.8 to 14.4. Pooled mean: 57.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 57.3 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 4 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=9.6: B = 0.985.
Interval [55.6, 55.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (55.6) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (57.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
